patches.find: a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse exceeding this floor by 10×–100× would be reportable even at Level-2 reduction
patches.replace: a 10–13 µGal contact-phase pulse exceeding this floor by 10×–130× would be reportable even at Level-2 reduction
patches.rationale: WIN-014.c6 H5 (minor): ratio computation 10-13 µGal / 0.1-1 µGal floor gives minimum 10/1=10×, maximum 13/0.1=130×. The text's '10×–100×' understates the upper bound by ~30%. Corrected to '10×–130×' per strict computation.

patches.find: No such identification has been offered; no published English-language contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located. The dome cannot simultaneously claim
patches.replace: No such identification has been offered; no published English-language contact-phase residual analysis from the 2009 campaign has been located. If the dome maintains that the contact-phase pulse appears only in Level-3 narrow-band residuals and not in standard broadband Level-2 reduction, the burden extends to specifying the carrier frequency, bandwidth, and coherence length required to isolate the predicted signal — none of these have been published. The dome cannot simultaneously claim
patches.rationale: WIN-014.c6 H4 (moderate, preemptive_recommendation): adds explicit counter to dome's strongest remaining defense — that the 2009 campaign used Level-2 reduction and PRED-TIER-3 specifies Level-3. The added sentence closes this loophole by requiring dome to specify the narrow-band parameters.
